comprehensive_summary:
August Strindberg is a Swedish playwright and novelist, best known for the plays Miss Julie, The Father, A Dream Play, and The Ghost Sonata. He is one of the most frequently clued dramatists in quizbowl.

Miss Julie is by far Strindberg's most clued work. Set on Midsummer's Eve, it follows the Count's daughter Miss Julie and the valet Jean. Julie breaks off her engagement when her fiance refuses to jump over a riding whip. She flirts with Jean, who cheats on the cook Christine/Kristine. Julie and Jean plot to open a hotel on Lake Como, but Jean decapitates Julie's pet canary (or greenfinch) with a razor. Julie's father the Count returns, and Jean gives Julie the razor with which she presumably commits suicide offstage. Julie tells Jean about a recurring dream of sitting on a pillar but finding no rest. The preface to Miss Julie is a naturalist manifesto: it calls the title character a 'man-hating half-woman,' discusses Jean's 'slave mentality' expressed through worship of the Count (symbolized by the Count's boots present on stage), and criticizes canvas stage doors for not being capable of expressing the 'anger of an irate pater familias.' Strindberg intended the title role for his wife Siri von Essen. Julie orders the cook to prepare an abortifacient for her dog Diana. A character makes up a story about a 'Turkish pavilion' and attempting suicide in an oat bin.

The Father features Laura goading her military officer husband (the Captain) into throwing a burning lamp at her, so she can have him declared insane and control the career of their daughter Bertha. Laura tells a lie about the Captain using a microscope (rather than a spectroscope) to discover extraterrestrial life. The Captain dies from a stroke after being put in a straitjacket. Strindberg wrote The Father as a response to Ibsen's A Doll's House, arguing that Nora is the true villain.

A Dream Play features Agnes, the daughter of the god Indra, descending to Earth to experience human hardships. The phrase 'men are to be pitied' is frequently repeated. Settings include Foulstrand, Fairhaven, and Fingal's Cave. A rapidly aging Officer pines for the opera singer Victoria. The deans of four university faculties argue about whether a door should be opened. A scene changes from a lawyer's office to a commencement ceremony. The play ends with a castle burning and a flower bud on its roof bursting into a giant chrysanthemum. A character describes stealing apples and entering a building that turns out to be an outhouse.

The Ghost Sonata is a chamber play set in a boarding house home to the wheelchair-bound Jacob Hummel and the student Arkenholtz ('Sunday child' who can see apparitions, including a milkmaid). An ogre-like cook sucks nourishment from food. Amalia (a parrot-like madwoman/mummy) convinces Hummel to hang himself. Arkenholtz's love interest (the Girl/Hyacinth Girl) dies behind a 'death screen' as Arkenholtz sings. Ingmar Bergman controversially depicted Hummel as Jewish. It is one of five three-scene 'chamber plays' premiered at the Intima Theater.

The Red Room is Strindberg's novel about the journalist Arvid Falk, who meets with bohemian artists and intellectuals at the titular location. The Triton marine insurance company turns out to be fraudulent. Falk's friend the painter Sellen loses his idealism.

Other works: The Stronger consists of a monologue by Frau X as Mademoiselle Y (revealed as Amelia) remains silent. The Pelican: Frederik commits arson. The Dance of Death: Alice hopes to be murdered during husband Edgar's saber dance. The Great Highway is Strindberg's final play, following a Hunter through seven 'stations' of life. Inferno is an autobiographical novel about practicing alchemy at the Hotel Orfila and believing oneself in hell. Married led to a blasphemy trial.

Biographically, Strindberg had a bitter rivalry with Henrik Ibsen -- he kept a portrait of Ibsen in his study and called it 'Madness Incipient.' He wrote 'Psychic Murder (Apropos Rosmersholm),' calling Rebecca West an 'unconscious cannibal.' Chekhov called him a misogynist 'who would like to see women enslaved.' He was virulently antisemitic, causing the critic Edvard Brandes to stop corresponding with him. He praised Otto Weininger's Sex and Character for 'solving the woman problem.' He wrote passionate letters to Nietzsche. Karl Jaspers wrote a 'pathographic analysis' of Strindberg and Van Gogh. He called himself 'the Zola of the Occult.' He founded the Intima Theater. Mauriac's Nobel lecture imagines the 'world of a novelist without hope' led by Strindberg.

works.name: Miss Julie
works.description: Set on Midsummer's Eve, the Count's daughter Julie flirts with the valet Jean. After Jean decapitates her pet canary, Julie commits suicide with a razor. The play's preface is a naturalist manifesto.

clues.clue: Ends with valet Jean giving Julie a razor; she commits suicide offstage
quotes: the valet Jean hands the title character a razor with which she presumably commits suicide offstage
the valet Jean gives the title character a razor to help her commit suicide

clues.clue: Jean decapitates Julie's pet canary (or greenfinch) with a razor
quotes: the man decapitates the woman's pet canary
the valet Jean cuts off the head of the title character's canary with a razor blade

clues.clue: Julie and Jean plan to open a hotel on Lake Como
quotes: two characters who plot to open a hotel in Lake Como
A woman created by this author wishes to open a hotel and build a 'villa by Lake Como'

clues.clue: Julie breaks off engagement when fiance refuses to jump over a riding whip
quotes: A character created by this man breaks off her engagement when her fiance refuses to jump over a riding whip

clues.clue: Count's boots present on stage; Jean's 'slave mentality'; the preface calls Julie a 'man-hating half-woman'
quotes: a discussion of the 'slave mentality' expressed by Jean's worship of the Count
This essay calls a work's title character a 'man-hating half-woman'

clues.clue: Jean cheats on the cook Christine/Kristine; Julie orders abortifacient for her dog Diana
quotes: After ordering her cook Kristine to prepare an abortifacient for her dog Diana

clues.clue: Julie's recurring dream of sitting on a pillar but finding no rest
quotes: Miss Julie tells Jean that she has a recurring dream in which she sits on one of these objects but 'can find no rest or peace'

clues.clue: A character makes up a story about a 'Turkish pavilion' and attempting suicide in an oat bin
quotes: Two characters dream about climbing up to a nest of golden eggs and a pillar before recounting a 'Turkish pavilion'
a man makes up a story about seeing a woman outside a 'Turkish pavilion' and attempting to commit suicide in an oat bin

clues.clue: Strindberg intended the role for his wife Siri von Essen; may have been inspired by Victoria Benedictsson's suicide
quotes: Strindberg intended the title character of this play for Siri von Essen

works.name: The Father
works.description: Laura tricks her Captain husband into throwing a burning lamp at her to have him declared insane. She lies about his using a microscope instead of a spectroscope. Written as response to Ibsen's A Doll's House.

clues.clue: Laura goads the Captain into throwing a burning lamp at her; he is declared insane and put in a straitjacket
quotes: a woman goads her military officer husband into throwing a burning lamp at her, so she can have him committed as insane
Laura reveals how Jacob... causing him to throw a lamp at her

clues.clue: Laura lies about the Captain using a microscope (really a spectroscope) to discover extraterrestrial life
quotes: A character in a play by this author tells a lie about her husband's attempt to use a microscope to discover extraterrestrial life

clues.clue: Laura fights to control daughter Bertha's career; Captain dies from stroke after straitjacket
quotes: The title captain's wife wants her daughter stay at home rather than go to town to become a teacher
Captain dies from a stroke after being put in a straitjacket because he grabbed a revolver

clues.clue: Written as response to Ibsen's A Doll's House; argues Nora is the true villain
quotes: One of these authors wrote The Father as a response to a play by the other, arguing that Nora, and not Torvald, is the true villain

clues.clue: Opens with debate over who is the father of a kitchen maid's illegitimate child; 'too many women running this house'
quotes: That play by this author opens with a debate over who the father of a kitchen maid's illegitimate child is

works.name: A Dream Play
works.description: Agnes, daughter of the god Indra, descends to Earth. Features Foulstrand, Fairhaven, and Fingal's Cave. Repeats 'men are to be pitied.' Ends with a castle burning and a chrysanthemum blooming.

clues.clue: Agnes, daughter of Indra, descends to Earth; 'men are to be pitied' repeated throughout
quotes: Agnes, the daughter of Indra, returns to heaven after experiencing human hardships
'men are to be pitied' is frequently repeated

clues.clue: Settings include Foulstrand, Fairhaven, and Fingal's Cave
quotes: A rapidly aging army officer... attempts to reach Fairhaven, only to end up in the hellish Foulstrand
includes the settings of Foulstrand, Fairhaven, and Fingal's Cave

clues.clue: Castle burning; flower bud bursts into giant chrysanthemum on the roof
quotes: a giant chrysanthemum bursts from a flower bud on the roof of a castle built on a dung-heap
a castle burning and the flower bud on its roof bursting into a giant chrysanthemum

clues.clue: Four university deans argue about whether a door should be opened; scene changes from lawyer's office to commencement
quotes: the deans of four university faculties argue about whether or not a door should be opened

quotes: the Officer is asked 'Do you think that time and space exist?'

works.name: The Ghost Sonata
works.description: A chamber play featuring the wheelchair-bound Hummel, the 'Sunday child' student Arkenholtz, and the Mummy. Set in a boarding house. One of five chamber plays for the Intima Theater.

clues.clue: Wheelchair-bound Jacob Hummel and student Arkenholtz in a boarding house; Arkenholtz is a 'Sunday child' who sees apparitions
quotes: set in a boarding house that is home to the wheelchair-bound Hummel and the student Arkenholtz

clues.clue: Amalia (the Mummy) convinces Hummel to hang himself; ogre-like cook sucks nourishment from food

quotes: Ingmar Bergman controversially depicted the wheelchair-bound Jacob Hummel as Jewish

works.name: The Red Room
works.description: Novel about journalist Arvid Falk who meets bohemian artists and intellectuals at the titular location. Features the fraudulent Triton marine insurance company.

clues.clue: Journalist Arvid Falk meets bohemian artists at the title location

the protagonist returns from a voyage to find that all of his friends, including the painter Sellen, have lost their idealism

clues.clue: Triton marine insurance company turns out to be fraudulent

a flax merchant is ruined when he donates phony shares in the Triton marine insurance company

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Various other works including The Stronger, The Pelican, The Dance of Death, The Great Highway, Inferno, and Married.

clues.clue: The Stronger: monologue by Frau X while Mademoiselle Y (Amelia) remains silent
quotes: Mademoiselle Y's name is revealed to be Amelia in one play by this author consisting of a monologue by Frau X

clues.clue: The Dance of Death: Alice hopes to be murdered during husband Edgar's saber dance
quotes: Alice hopes to be murdered during her husband Edgar's saber dance

clues.clue: The Great Highway: final play; Hunter through seven 'stations' of life
quotes: This author's final play features characters such as the Murderer and the Schoolmaster and describes the Hunter's journey through seven 'stations'

clues.clue: Inferno: autobiographical novel about practicing alchemy at Hotel Orfila; believes he is in hell
quotes: The narrator of a novel by this author practices alchemy at the Hotel Orfila and believes that he is living in hell

clues.clue: Married: short story collection that led to a blasphemy trial
quotes: This playwright of The Stronger faced a blasphemy trial over the publication of his short story collection Married

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Swedish playwright with a bitter rivalry with Ibsen. Called a misogynist by Chekhov. Founded the Intima Theater. Called himself 'the Zola of the Occult.'

clues.clue: Bitter rivalry with Ibsen: kept portrait of Ibsen called 'Madness Incipient'; wrote 'Psychic Murder (Apropos Rosmersholm)'
quotes: One of these authors kept a portrait of the other in his study and called it 'Madness Incipient'
called Rebecca West an 'unconscious cannibal, who has devoured Mrs. Rosmer's soul'

clues.clue: Chekhov called him a misogynist 'who would like to see women enslaved'
quotes: Anton Chekhov called this playwright a misogynist 'who would like to see women enslaved'

clues.clue: Founded the small Intima Theater for chamber plays
quotes: This playwright founded the small Intima Theater
Max Reinhardt inspired the use of this term for plays that Strindberg premiered in the 'Intimate Theater'

clues.clue: Virulently antisemitic; critic Edvard Brandes stopped writing to him
quotes: Strindberg's antisemitism led a Jewish critic of this surname to stop writing to him

clues.clue: Praised Weininger's Sex and Character for 'solving the woman problem'
quotes: This playwright claimed that Sex and Character had solved 'the woman problem'

clues.clue: Wrote passionate letters to Nietzsche ('Read Nietzsche! That is my Carthago est delenda!')
quotes: he declared, 'Read Nietzsche! That is my Carthago est delenda!'

clues.clue: Called himself 'the Zola of the Occult'
quotes: This self-proclaimed 'Zola of the Occult'

clues.clue: Karl Jaspers wrote a 'pathographic analysis' of Strindberg and Van Gogh
quotes: This author and Vincent Van Gogh are the subject of a 'pathographic analysis' by Karl Jaspers

clues.clue: Had a series of psychotic attacks known as his 'Inferno Crisis'
quotes: After a series of psychotic attacks known as his 'Inferno Crisis'

cards.front: Play: the valet Jean decapitates the title character's pet canary with a razor, then hands her the razor; she commits suicide offstage
Play: two characters plan to open a hotel on Lake Como; the title character breaks off her engagement when her fiance refuses to jump over a riding whip
Play: the preface calls the title character a 'man-hating half-woman' and discusses the valet's 'slave mentality' symbolized by the Count's boots on stage
Play: a character makes up a story about a 'Turkish pavilion' and attempting suicide in an oat bin; the title character dreams of sitting on a pillar
Play: Laura goads the Captain into throwing a burning lamp at her; she lies about his using a microscope (really a spectroscope) to find extraterrestrial life
Play: Laura has the Captain declared insane to control daughter Bertha's career; he dies in a straitjacket; written as response to A Doll's House
Play: Agnes, daughter of Indra, descends to Earth; 'men are to be pitied' is repeated; settings include Foulstrand, Fairhaven, and Fingal's Cave
Play: ends with a castle burning and a chrysanthemum blooming on its roof; four university deans argue about opening a door
Play: the wheelchair-bound Jacob Hummel and 'Sunday child' student Arkenholtz in a boarding house; Arkenholtz sees the apparition of a milkmaid
Play: a Mummy convinces Hummel to hang himself; an ogre-like cook sucks nourishment from food; the Girl dies behind a 'death screen'
Novel: journalist Arvid Falk meets bohemian artists at the title location; the Triton marine insurance company is fraudulent
Play: Alice hopes to be murdered during her husband Edgar's saber dance
Playwright: kept a portrait of Ibsen called 'Madness Incipient'; called Rebecca West an 'unconscious cannibal' in 'Psychic Murder (Apropos Rosmersholm)'
Playwright: founded the Intima Theater for chamber plays; Chekhov called him a misogynist 'who would like to see women enslaved'
Playwright: wrote passionate letters to Nietzsche declaring 'That is my Carthago est delenda!'; called himself 'the Zola of the Occult'
Playwright: Karl Jaspers wrote a 'pathographic analysis' pairing him with Van Gogh; had a series of psychotic attacks known as the 'Inferno Crisis'

summary: August Strindberg is a Swedish playwright and novelist, best known for the plays Miss Julie, The Father, A Dream Play, and The Ghost Sonata.
